question: What's the best time to visit Gold Coast Queensland with kids?
answer: G'day! April to May and September to November are ripper times - you'll dodge the summer crowds and humidity while still enjoying beautiful beach weather. School holidays get busy, so book early! Winter (June-August) is still lovely with temps around 20-25°C, perfect for theme parks. Just remember, summer can be stormy but the kids won't mind splashing about in the holiday park pool!

question: Are there pet friendly holiday parks in Gold Coast Queensland?
answer: You bet! Plenty of Gold Coast holiday parks welcome your furry mates. Many have designated pet-friendly cabins or powered sites, though some areas are pet-free zones. You'll usually need to keep dogs on-leash and bring their bedding. Always ring ahead to check specific pet policies and any extra fees. Your pooch will love being part of the family holiday!

question: How much do holiday parks cost in Gold Coast Queensland during school holidays?
answer: School holiday prices typically range from $150-$400+ per night depending on accommodation type. Powered sites are cheapest ($50-$100), while premium cabins cost more ($250-$400+). Easter and Christmas are priciest. Book 6-12 months ahead for better rates and availability. Off-peak visits can save you a motza - sometimes half the price of peak periods!

question: What should I pack for a holiday park stay with young kids?
answer: Don't forget swimmers, rash vests, sunscreen (SPF50+), hats, and beach towels! Pack insect repellent, a first aid kit, and any favourite toys. Most cabins have kitchens, so bring basic groceries. Stroller, portable high chair, and night lights are handy. Bring bikes or scooters - kids love riding around the park. Check if your park provides linen or BYO!

question: What is there to do near Gold Coast Queensland holiday parks for families?
answer: Heaps! You've got world-class theme parks (Dreamworld, Sea World, Movie World), stunning beaches, and Currumbin Wildlife Sanctuary. Most holiday parks have pools, playgrounds, and jumping pillows on-site. Explore the hinterland for rainforest walks, visit local markets, or try mini golf. Many parks organize kids' activities during holidays. There's honestly something for every age!
